title: epacris corymbiflora
intro: Epacris corymbiflora, also known as the bell heath or bell heather, is a species of flowering plant native to Australia. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Aboriginal medicine, where it is valued for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant has been found to have health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. In herbal preparations, Epacris corymbiflora is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
